name: Juan Carlos Cebrian Amasifen
position: Professor Doctor, Department of Electrical Energy and Automation
department: Department of Electrical Energy and Automation Engineering
short_bio: Juan Carlos Cebrian Amasifen is a professor at the University of São Paulo, Escola Politécnica, Department of Electrical Energy and Automation Engineering. His research focuses on power quality, distribution network planning, smart grids, Industry 4.0, Internet of Things, distributed energy resources, and optimization methods applied to modern electric power systems.
long_bio: Juan Carlos Cebrian Amasifen received his BSc in Mechanical-Electrical Engineering from the Universidad Nacional de Ingeniería, Peru, his MSc in Electrical Engineering from São Paulo State University, Brazil, and his PhD in Electrical Engineering from the University of São Paulo. He carried out postdoctoral research at USP, UNICAMP, and UFABC, with international research stays at the University of Manchester and Virginia Tech. His research interests include power quality, distribution system planning, smart grids, Industry 4.0, Internet of Things, distributed energy resources, and optimization techniques.
image_alt: Portrait of Juan Carlos Cebrian Amasifen

name: Gustavo Lino Morales Espinoza
department: Department of Electrical Energy and Automation Engineering
short_bio: Gustavo Lino Morales Espinoza is a PhD researcher at the University of São Paulo, working on distribution network planning, hosting capacity, power quality, distributed energy resources, photovoltaic systems, battery energy storage, and optimization methods applied to electric power systems.
long_bio: Gustavo Lino Morales Espinoza is a researcher associated with the University of São Paulo and Cebrian Lab. His research focuses on distribution expansion planning, hosting capacity assessment, smart grids, power quality, distributed photovoltaic generation, battery energy storage systems, and optimization techniques such as genetic algorithms and Monte Carlo simulation. His recent work includes methodologies for PV allocation, hosting capacity assessment with PV and BESS, and distribution expansion planning considering reliability indices, voltage sags, power interruptions, and sensitive industrial customers.

education.institution: Universidad Nacional de San Agustín de Arequipa

image_alt: Portrait of Gustavo Lino Morales Espinoza

name: Carlos Alberto Carneiro
position: Professor and PhD Researcher in Electrical Engineering
secondary_affiliation: Centro Estadual de Educação Tecnológica Paula Souza
short_bio: Carlos Alberto Carneiro is an external collaborator associated with Cebrian Lab, working on power distribution systems, distributed generation, power quality, automated switch allocation, and optimization applied to distribution networks.
long_bio: Carlos Alberto Carneiro holds a Master's degree in Electrical Engineering from São Paulo State University, with research focused on automated switch allocation in power distribution networks using a multiobjective approach. He is currently pursuing doctoral research in Electrical Engineering and works as a professor at Centro Estadual de Educação Tecnológica Paula Souza in industrial automation and electrotechnics. His research interests include power distribution systems, distributed generation, power quality, voltage sags, power interruptions, reliability indicators, and optimization methods applied to distribution networks.

education.research_topic: Automated switch allocation considering distributed energy resources in power distribution networks

education.thesis: Alocação de chaves automatizadas em redes elétricas de distribuição: uma abordagem multiobjetivo

education.institution: Faculdade de Ciências Humanas de Aguaí

name: Rennard de Oliveira Brito
department: Department of Electrical Energy and Automation Engineering
short_bio: Rennard de Oliveira Brito is an MSc researcher at the University of São Paulo, working on distribution network expansion planning using OpenDSS, Python-based frameworks, and evolutionary algorithms.
long_bio: Rennard de Oliveira Brito is an MSc researcher at the University of São Paulo (USP), Escola Politécnica. His research focuses on distribution network expansion planning with OpenDSS and Python-based frameworks, applying NSGA-II methods to address energy losses, reverse power flow, and voltage violations in Brazilian distribution systems. He also works at Enel Brasil on the technical losses team, contributing to Python tools for network analysis and optimization.
image_alt: Portrait of Rennard de Oliveira Brito

name: David Koller Filiu
department: Department of Electrical Energy and Automation Engineering
short_bio: David Koller Filiu is a PhD researcher at the University of São Paulo, working on power quality, short-duration voltage variations, Impact Factor estimation, and financial compensation analysis in electrical distribution networks.
long_bio: David Koller Filiu is a PhD researcher at the University of São Paulo (USP). His research focuses on Power Quality, short-duration voltage variations (VTCD), and the estimation and minimization of financial compensation based on violations in the Impact Factor of VTCDs in electrical distribution networks. He develops Monte Carlo-based methodologies using OpenDSS and Python frameworks to address power quality violations in Brazilian distribution networks.

name: Jairo Giacomini Jr
short_bio: Jairo Giacomini Jr is a PhD researcher associated with Cebrian Lab, working on power distribution systems, power quality, distributed energy resources, voltage regulation, and consensus-based control methods.
long_bio: Jairo Giacomini Jr is a PhD researcher associated with Cebrian Lab. His research focuses on power distribution systems, power quality, voltage sags, photovoltaic integration, distributed energy resources, energy storage systems, and consensus-based algorithms for voltage regulation and active power sharing in distribution networks and microgrids.

name: Gabriela Batista Silva
short_bio: Gabriela Batista Silva is a researcher associated with Cebrian Lab, working on power distribution systems, power system reliability, self-healing networks, consumer prioritization, and artificial intelligence applied to electrical power systems.
long_bio: Gabriela Batista Silva is an electrical engineer and researcher associated with Cebrian Lab. Her research focuses on the use of artificial intelligence and multi-criteria decision-making methods to improve the reliability of power distribution systems, especially in self-healing network restoration and consumer prioritization during outage events.
